explicit_text: I've worked at Harborview Medical Center since 2007, starting as a registered nurse in trauma surgical acute care and later moving to the trauma surgical intensive care units. Harborview has been my first and only nursing job since I got my license. During the COVID-19 pandemic, Harborview converted units to COVID ICUs where I worked extensively, especially as an ECMO specialist. The hospital's response to PPE and safety has been better than many others, partly due to our union's active involvement.

explicit_text: I am an ECMO specialist, which means I help run the extracorporeal membrane oxygenation machine, an advanced life support system that can perform lung or heart-lung bypass. This role has been especially critical during the COVID-19 pandemic when we saw much higher volumes of patients needing this therapy. ECMO requires constant monitoring during a 12-hour shift, and it’s one of the most intense therapies we offer.

explicit_text: I have been a member of SEIU 1199 since I started at Harborview in 2007, though I became more active about two years ago. I serve as a union delegate and have taken on roles such as interim co-chair for the nurse staffing committee and chairing joint labor management meetings. During the COVID-19 pandemic, our union has been very active in bargaining over PPE, furloughs, and redeployment, which has helped ensure we have proper protections and a voice in hospital policies.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed my work at Harborview, where I shifted mostly to COVID intensive care units that didn't exist before. The pandemic brought intense challenges like wearing full PPE, managing critically ill patients on ECMO, and dealing with mental and emotional exhaustion. It also highlighted disparities in patient access to communication and increased tensions around public perceptions, including frustration with the federal response and misinformation.

explicit_text: As an Asian-American nurse, I felt a deep frustration when I heard the term 'Chinese virus' used by the president during the pandemic. It was hurtful and contributed to a difficult social environment amid an already challenging time. This rhetoric affected not only me but also the broader community and complicated efforts to present a united front against COVID-19.
